title: Prefer using secrets as files over secrets as environment variables

input_row.title: Prefer using secrets as files over secrets as environment variables
input_row.description: Kubernetes supports mounting secrets as data volumes or as environment variables. Minimize the use of environment variable secrets.
input_row.rationale: It is reasonably common for application code to log out its environment (particularly in the event of an error). This will include any secret values passed in as environment variables, so secrets can easily be exposed to any user or entity who has access to the logs.
input_row.audit: Run the following command to find references to objects which use environment variables defined from secrets. kubectl get all -o jsonpath='{range .items[?(@..secretKeyRef)]} {.kind} {.metadata.name} {"\n"}{end}' -A
input_row.remediation: If possible, rewrite application code to read secrets from mounted secret files, rather than from environment variables.
input_row.impact: Application code which expects to read secrets in the form of environment variables would need modification
input_row.default_value: By default, secrets are not defined
input_row.additional_information: Mounting secrets as volumes has the additional benefit that secret values can be updated without restarting the pod Internal Only - General

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**APPROACH DECISION:**
- The audit command provided in the control can be executed using `kubectl`, which allows us to query the Kubernetes API for objects that use environment variables defined from secrets.
- The rationale highlights a security concern that can be evaluated through the Kubernetes API, specifically regarding how secrets are utilized within the cluster.
- The control does not require subjective review or access to the host OS, as it can be fully assessed through Kubernetes API calls.

**PROGRAM NAME:** k8s_pod_environment_variables_secrets_restricted

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- Use the command: `kubectl get all -o jsonpath='{range .items[?(@..secretKeyRef)]} {.kind} {.metadata.name} {"\n"}{end}' -A`
- This command queries all namespaces for Kubernetes objects that reference secrets via environment variables.
- The specific resources to enumerate include all Kubernetes objects (pods, deployments, etc.) that may utilize secrets as environment variables.
- Pass/Fail criteria:
  - **Pass:** No objects are returned by the command, indicating that there are no secrets being used as environment variables.
  - **Fail:** One or more objects are returned, indicating that secrets are being used as environment variables, which is not preferred according to the control.
- The expected configuration is that secrets should be mounted as files rather than being passed as environment variables.
